guest_role: Co-founder and managing partner of Electric Capital (crypto-native VC fund)
assertiveness_why: Harry asks challenging questions (whether extrapolating exponentially means investing in everything, questioning tourist participation) and expresses skepticism on crypto hype culture, but remains mostly receptive without planting counter-theses.
question_style: Exploratory with occasional skeptical pushback; Harry admits knowledge gaps ('I knew very little') but asks probing follow-ups; occasionally challenges logic without offering alternatives; conversational and engaged.
tone: Warm and curious with strategic skepticism; Harry acknowledges Avachal's passion as authentic while pushing back on narratives he finds concerning (hype, tourism); balanced between learning and mild critique.
depth: Technical deep-dive into crypto infrastructure (custody, tax, audits, DEXs, smart contracts), DAO governance as economic/political experiments, token economics (ownership concentration constraints), organizational design for crypto investing (50% engineers), capital markets disruption thesis, exponential growth intuitions, geopolitical settlement layers.
harry_quotes: Were you nervous about making the move to institutional?
Why does it allow it to be much more collaborative?
Can you unpack software eating capital markets for me?
What does it mean to extrapolate exponentially in reality?
Does it make your job harder having tourists in the space?
learnings: Crypto networks require dispersed ownership (low single-digit percentages per investor) to maintain protocol resilience and community adoption; concentrated VC ownership triggers 'organ rejection' by users.
Software eating capital markets: programmable code-based ownership replaces 1970s infrastructure (NDAs, PDFs, Excel) with on-chain governance; enables 24/7 global capital markets.

Crypto VC requires fundamentally different organizational DNA: 50% engineers on staff, no traditional associates/principals hierarchy; processes built around Discord discovery, code audits, custody infrastructure building.
Token vs equity: if company dissolves and token retains value, company existed in service of the network (token primary); if token requires ongoing company, equity is primary value capture.
DAO experiments represent run-able economic and governance models at scale (millions in parallel); 7-10 years needed to determine which governance structures prove durable.

Crypto pricing uses venture logic (what could it be worth × required multiple = underwriting), not NPV; institutional scale token buying requires custom infrastructure (OTC desks, DEX access, tax software, custody code).

Venture intuitions on market size are systematically wrong by 1-2 orders of magnitude; exponential growth extrapolation (internet, mobile, cloud) historically correct; crypto likely 10-100X bigger than consensus predicts.

Ethereum could become credibly neutral digital settlement layer for third geopolitical sphere (non-US, non-China) with multi-trillion-dollar GDP if extrapolated exponentially; distributed governance creates neutrality unlike traditional governments.
repeat_guest_callback: Yes: 'We did one many years ago'
notable: Electric raised $1B fund; founder argues crypto fundamentally disrupts venture capital through code-based capital deployment; organizational design must shift (50% engineers, no associates); Ethereum could function as credibly neutral digital country for settlement between geopolitical spheres.
